summary: Marcelo Bielsa's Uruguay — aggressive, intense and brave against the biggest sides. All-out tactics built on hard running and fearless man-to-man marking, swarming the ball and crunching into tackles, but able to sink into a deep block and counter. They beat world champions Argentina in November 2023 with this mix.
style: Shapeshifting around a 5-3-2 — midfielders follow pass receivers man-to-man, wide centre-backs step out to plug gaps, and wingers (Pellistri, Maxi Araújo) drop to full-back to form a back five. Commit men high to force turnovers (most high turnovers in South American qualifying), or sink into a barricaded back-seven to ride pressure and break.

counterReliance.note: Aggressive man-to-man press + counter; can also defend deep and break

strengths: Relentless pressing and man-to-man intensity — forces the most high turnovers in CONMEBOL
Tactical flexibility — swarm high or barricade deep, then counter
Midfield grit and physical conditioning to sustain the energy
weaknesses: Without Cavani and Suárez for the first time since 2006 — less attacking firepower
Throwing men forward leaves space if a pass is misplaced or a runner doesn't recover

keyPlayers.name: Maxi Araújo
keyPlayers.note: Fired-up box-to-box presence — aggressive 1v1 defender and powerful runner who times late runs and strikes left-footed; the engine of the wide press

keyPlayers.name: Rodrigo Bentancur
keyPlayers.note: Storms out to press and anchors the man-to-man scheme that defines Bielsa's shape

tactics: Press man-to-man and swarm the ball to win it high, or drop into a deep block to draw teams on and counter; flood the centre when in control. Always among the most exciting watches — but the aggression opens spaces, so they must be cautious against pace, especially vs Spain in the final group game.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group H (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
